ddls:
CREATE TABLE "companies" (
  "company_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each company */,
  "rank" INTEGER /* Ranking of the company in the Fortune 500 list */,
  "title" TEXT /* Name of the company */,
  "revenues" TEXT /* Annual revenues in millions of dollars */,
  "profits" TEXT /* Annual profits in millions of dollars */,
  "employees" INTEGER /* Number of employees */,
  "sector_id" INTEGER /* Sector in which the company operates */,
  "industry_id" INTEGER /* Industry classification of the company */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude coordinate of the company's headquarters */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude coordinate of the company's headquarters */,
  "founded_year" INTEGER /* Year the company was founded */,
  "ceo_name" TEXT /* Name of the CEO */,
  "ceo_tenure_start" INTEGER /* Year the current CEO started their tenure */,
  "market_cap" TEXT /* Market capitalization of the company in billions of dollars */,
  "website" TEXT /* Official website of the company */,
  "headquarters_address" TEXT /* Address of the company's headquarters */,
  "description" TEXT /* Brief description of the company */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Main contact phone number */,
  "email" TEXT /* Main contact email */,
  "stock_symbol" TEXT /* Stock symbol of the company */,
  "exchange" TEXT /* Stock exchange where the company is listed */,
  "rating" TEXT /* Rating or score of the company */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the company's record */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("company_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_companies_industry_id FOREIGN KEY ("industry_id") REFERENCES industries ("industry_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_companies_sector_id FOREIGN KEY ("sector_id") REFERENCES sectors ("sector_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sectors" (
  "sector_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sector */,
  "sector_name" TEXT /* Name of the sector */,
  "description" TEXT /* Brief description of the sector */,
  "parent_sector_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parent sector (for hierarchical sector classification) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sector_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "industries" (
  "industry_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each industry */,
  "industry_name" TEXT /* Name of the industry */,
  "description" TEXT /* Brief description of the industry */,
  "parent_industry_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parent industry (for hierarchical industry classification) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("industry_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "financials" (
  "financial_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each financial record */,
  "company_id" INTEGER /* ID of the company */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year for the financial data */,
  "revenues" TEXT /* Revenues in millions of dollars for that year */,
  "profits" TEXT /* Profits in millions of dollars for that year */,
  "assets" TEXT /* Total assets in millions of dollars */,
  "liabilities" TEXT /* Total liabilities in millions of dollars */,
  "equity" TEXT /* Shareholder equity in millions of dollars */,
  "cash_flow" TEXT /* Operating cash flow in millions of dollars */,
  "debt" TEXT /* Total debt in millions of dollars */,
  "eps" TEXT /* Earnings per share */,
  "pe_ratio" TEXT /* Price-to-earnings ratio */,
  "dividend_yield" TEXT /* Dividend yield as a percentage */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the financial record */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("financial_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_financials_company_id FOREIGN KEY ("company_id") REFERENCES companies ("company_id")
)
column_names: Ranking of the company in the Fortune 500 list
Annual revenues in millions of dollars
Annual profits in millions of dollars
Sector in which the company operates
